subject: Geology
title: Crystal Structure
description: Learn Crystal Structure in one minute.

hook.text: A diamond and graphite are both pure carbon. One is the hardest mineral; the other crumbles in your hand.

buildup.text: The difference is crystal structure: how atoms are arranged in a repeating three-dimensional pattern.

discovery.text: Diamond's carbon atoms form rigid tetrahedra. Graphite's atoms form loose, slippery sheets that slide apart.

twist.text: Crystal structure determines nearly everything: hardness, shape, cleavage, and even color of a mineral.

climax.text: Same atoms, different arrangement, completely different mineral. Structure matters more than composition.

punchline.text: Arrangement turns pencil lead into diamond.

quiz.question: Why are diamond and graphite so different?
options: Their atoms are arranged in different crystal structures
